person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
see also LDSW-1WG   who is same but on a different tree

Matilda of Swabia (German: Mathilde von Schwaben; c.?988/989-29 July 1032), a member of the Conradine dynasty, was Duchess of Carinthia by her first marriage with Duke Conrad I and Duchess of Upper Lorraine by her second marriage to Duke Frederick II. She played an active role in promoting her son, Duke Conrad the Younger, as a candidate for the German throne in 1024 and to this end corresponded with King Mieszko II of Poland.

«b»Family«/b»
Matilda was the daughter of Duke Herman II of Swabia (d. 1003) and his wife Gerberga (c.965/966-1019), a daughter of King Conrad I of Burgundy. She had many illustrious relatives. Through her father, Matilda was descended from the Ottonian king Henry the Fowler; through her mother from King Louis IV of France and Charlemagne.

After the death of Emperor Otto III in 1002, Matilda's father, Duke Herman, opposed the election of her cousin Duke Henry IV of Bavaria as German king, and promoted himself as a rival candidate for the throne. Herman and Henry both claimed descent from Henry the Fowler, progenitor of the Ottonian dynasty.

«b»Life«/b»
About 1001/02 Matilda married Conrad of Carinthia, son of Duke Otto I, a member of the Salian dynasty. Conrad supported her father's bid for the German throne in 1002. Their marriage was possibly consanguineous and therefore was condemned by Henry II (her father's rival who was now crowned German king) at the Synod of Thionville in January 1003. A heated debate broke out; nevertheless, the couple remained together until Conrad's death in 1011.

After Duke Conrad died, his minor son with Matilda, Conrad the Younger, was passed over in the succession for the Carinthian duchy. Instead King Henry II ceded the duchy to Count Adalbero of Eppenstein, who was married to Matilda's sister, Beatrice. Matilda had Conrad the Younger placed in the care of one of his Salian relative Conrad the Elder (the future king Conrad II Germany). A few years later (c.1016/7), Matilda's sister Gisela of Swabia married Conrad the Elder. Matilda maintained good relations with the couple. In 1019, her brother-in-law supported her son, Conrad the Younger, when he tried to reclaim Carinthia from Duke Adalbero. However, the attempt was unsuccessful and possibly caused Conrad the Elder to go into exile.

About 1012/13, Matilda herself had married her second husband, Count Frederick of Bar, the son of Duke Theodoric I of Upper Lorraine. This marriage was also consanguineous. Frederick succeeded his father in 1019; he is usually said to have died c. 1026, although it is possible that he lived until 1033.

The Salian unity came to an end, when in 1024 Emperor Henry II died without heirs: both Matilda's brother-in-law Conrad the Elder and her son Conrad the Younger promoted themselves as candidates for the throne as descendants of Henry the Fowler. Conrad the Elder was elected King of the Romans (as Conrad II) at an assembly at Kamba (near Oppenheim) in Rhenish Franconia on 4 September 1024. Conrad the Younger refused to accept the new king and his mother Matilda, with her second husband Frederick and his Lorraine entourage, left the site in protest. Duke Frederick continued to support Conrad the Younger, as did Conrad's cousin, Duke Ernest II of Swabia.

Matilda herself remained active on her son's behalf. Between 1025 and 1027 she opened negotiations with the new Polish king Mieszko II Lambert, who was also at odds with Conrad the Elder (King Conrad II), as he refused to recognise Mieszko as king and even demanded the Polish regalia from him. While Mieszko's rule was not only questioned by Conrad but also by his own Piast relatives, Matilda presented him with a valuable liturgical manuscript (the Liber de Officiis divinis). The dedicatory page of the book contained a letter from Matilda to Mieszko (Epistola ad Mathildis Suevae Misegonem II Poloniae Regem) in which she named him a distinguished king, praised him for his building of new churches, and knowledge of Latin, and wished him strength against his enemies. The dedicatory page also contained a miniature depicting Matilda giving the book to Mieszko, who is shown wearing a crown and seated on a throne.

Matilda's gift had the desired effect, and Mieszko promised to take military action. Several clashes of arms followed; by 1028, however, Emperor Conrad II had defeated all his opponents. By 1030 Matilda seems to have been on good terms with Conrad II again. She joined him and her sister, Empress Gisela, at the imperial court at Ingelheim in Easter 1030. In 1035, Emperor Conrad II finally deprived Adalbero of Carinthia, for rebelling against him, and restored Conrad the Younger to the duchy.

Following an entry in the Annalista Saxo, Matilda is sometimes said to have married a third time, c.1026, to Count Esico of Ballenstedt. She thereby would be the progenitor of the Saxon House of Ascania; nevertheless, this is not possible if her first husband Frederick lived until 1033.

«b»Death«/b»
Matilda died sometime after Easter 1030 (when she was at the imperial court) and before January 1034, when Emperor Conrad II issued a diploma at the intervention of his wife Gisela, commemorating her death. She is often said to have died on 29 July 1032. She was buried in Worms Cathedral. After Matilda's death, her young daughters (Beatrice and Sophie) from her second marriage to Frederick were adopted by her sister, Empress Gisela.

«b»Issue«/b»
With her first husband, Conrad, Matilda had three children:

1.) Conrad II, Duke of Carinthia

2.) Bishop Bruno of Würzburg

3.) Gisela (?), who married Count Gerhard of Metz, whose brother was Bruno of Toul (later Pope Gregory V).

With her second husband, Frederick, Matilda had three children:

1.) Frederick III, Duke of Upper Lorraine

2.) Beatrice of Bar

3.) Sophie, Countess of Bar.

If she was married to Esico of Ballenstedt, Matilda had two further children:

1.) Adalbert II, Count of Ballenstedt

2.) Adelaide of Ballenstadt, wife of Thiemo of Schraplau.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen於從0988年到0989年出生在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 索斯特維爾時，她的父親Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben40歲，母親Gerberge de Bourgogne22歲。她於1012年與Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1032年7月29日在德國薩辛-安哈爾特可德林波格巴倫斯泰特，埋葬在德國海藍-普法爾茲喬姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen于从0988年到0989年出生在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 索斯特維爾时，她的父亲Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben40岁，母亲Gerberge de Bourgogne22岁。她于1012年与Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1032年7月29日在德国薩辛-安哈爾特可德林波格巴倫斯泰特，埋葬在德国海藍-普法爾茲喬姆斯。

meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen was born from 0988 to 0989, in Werl, Soest, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, her father, Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben, was 40 and her mother, Gerberge de Bourgogne, was 22. She married Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie in 1012. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 29 July 1032, in Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany, and was buried in Worms, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen est née du 0988 au 0989, à Werl, Kreis Soest, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, son père, Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Gerberge de Bourgogne, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie en 1012. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 juillet 1032, à Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Allemagne, et a été enterrée à Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen von 0988 bis 0989 in Werl, Kreis Soest, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben 40 und ihre Mutter Gerberge de Bourgogne 22. Sie hat 1012 Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 29. Juli 1032 in Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Deutschland und wurde in Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen è nata il da 0988 a 0989 nel Werl, Kreis Soest, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, sua padre, Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Gerberge de Bourgogne, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie nel 1012. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 29 luglio 1032, nel Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Germania, e fu sepolta a Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringenがから0988年まで0989年にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレイス ソエストウエルルで生まれた時，父Hermann II. Herzog von Schwabenは40歳，母Gerberge de Bourgogneは22歳であった。1012年にFrédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1032年7月29日にドイツサチセン-アンハルトクエドリンブルグボーレンステトで死去し，ドイツルヘインランド-プファルズウオルムズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen(이)가 0988년 부터 0989년 까지 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 크레이스 소스트 워에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Gerberge de Bourgogne(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1012년 Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1032년 7월 29일 독일 작센-아할트 크들린버그 바렌스테드트에서 사망했으며 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 워스에 묻혔다.

lv.von 0988 bis 0989–29. Juli 1032: no 0988. gada līdz 0989. gadam–1032. gada 29. jūlijs

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen nasceu de 0988 a 0989, em Werl, Kreis Soest, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, seu pai, Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Gerberge de Bourgogne, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie em 1012. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 29 de julho de 1032, em Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Alemanha, e foi sepultada em Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen родилась с 0988 по 0989 в местности вирл, крис соест, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия, её отцу по имени Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Gerberge de Bourgogne, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie в 1012 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 29 июля 1032 г. в местности балленстедт, куедлинбург, сачсен-анэлт, Германия и была похоронена в местности вормс, раинланд-пфалз, Германия.

sm.von 0988 bis 0989–29. Juli 1032: mai le 0988 e o'o I le 0989–29 Iulai 1032
sm.von 0988 bis 0989: mai le 0988 e o'o I le 0989

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathilde von Schwaben von Ober-Lothringen nació desde 0988 hasta 0989, en Werl, Kreis Soest, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, su papá, Hermann II. Herzog von Schwaben, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Gerberge de Bourgogne, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Frédéric II de Lorraine duc de Haute-Lotharingie en 1012. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 29 de julio de 1032, en Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Alemania, y fue sepultada en Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania.

vi.von 0988 bis 0989–29. Juli 1032: từ năm 0988 đến năm 0989–ngày 29 tháng 7 năm 1032
